highlight.title: BGP Vortex: The New Internet Vulnerability Making Headlines
highlight.summary: <cite index="11-1,11-14">A single BGP vortex can delay internet routing from almost instantaneous to up to 40 seconds, causing outages lasting around 37 seconds</cite>. <cite index="11-3,11-12">21 of the 30 largest internet networks are vulnerable to these attacks, and if exploited together, could impact 96% of all networks globally</cite>. This isn't theoretical—real outages like <cite index="12-1">Cloudflare's February 2026 BGP misconfiguration made services unreachable for roughly six hours</cite>.

resources.title: How DNS Works (Interactive Comic)
resources.why_now: <cite index="34-3,34-5">This colorful webcomic explains what happens when you type a website address in your browser</cite>. Perfect starting point for complete beginners to understand the internet's phone book.

resources.title: What Happens When You Type a URL? (Educative)
resources.why_now: <cite index="21-8,21-9">Covers the complete multi-step process from DNS resolution to TLS negotiation to rendering HTML, CSS, and JavaScript on screen</cite>. Updated March 2026 with modern examples including HTTP/3 and QUIC protocols.

resources.title: Computer Networks: A Systems Approach - Global Internet
resources.why_now: <cite index="9-11,9-24">Explains how BGP advertises complete paths between networks and prevents routing loops by carrying full AS paths in routing messages</cite>. Essential for understanding the technical details behind recent BGP outages.

resources.title: How Does the Internet Work? (Cloudflare)
resources.why_now: <cite index="2-4,2-27">Explains packet switching and the two fundamental concepts behind internet function: packets and protocols</cite>. <cite index="2-38,2-39">Uses the Statue of Liberty analogy to show how digital information travels in pieces across the world in milliseconds</cite>.

claude_take: The recent BGP vulnerabilities and outages show how fragile our internet infrastructure really is—major networks can disappear from the internet entirely due to simple configuration errors. Understanding these fundamentals isn't just academic curiosity; it's essential digital literacy for anyone working in tech or just wanting to understand why their favorite service suddenly goes dark for hours.
